subject: [RFC PATCH 1/9 v2] cgroup: Introduce memory_tiered_limits cgroup mount option

2026-04-23.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new cgroup mount option called memory_tiered_limits, which enables tier-proportional scaling of the memory cgroup controller limits. Currently, this option has no effect but will be used in later commits to scale memcg limits proportional to the system's toptier:total capacity ratio. The patch adds support for this new option by modifying several kernel files, including include/linux/cgroup-defs.h, include/linux/memcontrol.h, and kernel/cgroup/cgroup.c. The changes include adding a new flag to cgrp_dfl_root.flags and modifying the mem_cgroup_tiered_limits function to check if the memory_tiered_limits option is enabled.
